verdict.force_bf16_accum: RULED OUT — bf16 stepwise is farther from BOTH dumps (tgt rel 0.126); Heph is bit-exact f32-accum; HF is the one that differs from sequential f32 sum

verdict.heph_q_proj: bit-identical to sequential f32-accum of xn @ W^T
verdict.hf_q_proj: differs in 110/315392 elements (max abs 0.00195); torch Linear reduction order / blocked sum vs sequential
verdict.causal: those 110 elements alone flip logit 16.38 → 4.71 (hybrid inject)
verdict.one_line_fix_hint: Do NOT force bf16 accum (wrong direction). To match HF, match torch matmul reduction/blocking numerics for BF16×BF16→BF16 with f32 accum, or accept ULP sensitivity and treat residual as benign only if FP8 bar passes after other fixes. Practical options: use the same vendor matmul as torch/ROCm for prefill, or add a reduction-order that bit-matches torch on this shape.
verdict.note: inject_q_proj collapse is real but does not mean Heph matmul is mis-accumulated; it means HF and Heph disagree at ULP level and this row amplifies that disagreement.

hybrid_inject.interpretation: Changing only the 110 bf16 elements where Heph differs from HF is sufficient and necessary to collapse the spike (hybrid == full HF inject; heph-self inject reproduces control).
